metadata.title: Build a Quantum Harmonic Oscillator Ladder with Claude Code
metadata.color_semantics: claude: INK #3D3929 all text; SPARK #D97757 the one accent.
metadata.greeting_note: hello lexicon: Japanese. Medhavy 1-word budget. Wagwan is Bear-only.
metadata.audio_note: All existing inner-beat mp3s must be regenerated with kokoro af_kore (existing use voice_id 1sgY6Voq1aexKOB1IJ2D directly): python3 runtime/scripts/generate_audio.py quantum-mechanics-vol1/youtube/medhavy-vol1-harmonic-ladder

beats.narration_text: Let's recap with Claude. The prompt asks Claude Code for a Manim scene drawing the harmonic oscillator's parabola and first five equally spaced rungs. Before running, read for the line h-bar times omega times n plus one-half and confirm the one-half keeps the ground state off zero. Running it, the gap between every consecutive pair reads 0.066 electron-volts and the ground rung sits at 0.033 electron-volts. Iterating labels the uniform h-bar-omega spacing; the oscillator is the vehicle and the read-run loop is the takeaway.

artifactLines: The prompt asks Claude Code for a Manim scene drawing the harmonic oscillator's parabola and first five equally spaced rungs.
Before running, read for the line h-bar times omega times n plus one-half and confirm the one-half keeps the ground state off zero.
Running it, the gap between every consecutive pair reads 0.066 electron-volts and the ground rung sits at 0.033 electron-volts.
Iterating labels the uniform h-bar-omega spacing; the oscillator is the vehicle and the read-run loop is the takeaway.

beats.narration_text: Reading for the line h-bar times omega times n plus one-half, and confirming the one-half keeps the ground state off zero, made the check step feel non-optional. I want to extend this Manim scene: (1) give me the Claude Code prompt to overlay the Gaussian ground-state wavefunction on the parabola; (2) tell me the line I should read to confirm the spacing stays h-bar-omega; (3) show me the numeric check that doubling omega doubles both the 0.066 electron-volt gap and the 0.033 electron-volt ground energy.

props.segment: Build a Quantum Harmonic Oscillator Ladder with Claude Code
props.command: Reading for the line h-bar times omega times n plus one-half, and confirming the one-half keeps the ground state off zero, made the check step feel non-optional. I want to extend this Manim scene: (1) give me the Claude Code prompt to overlay the Gaussian ground-state wavefunction on the parabola; (2) tell me the line I should read to confirm the spacing stays h-bar-omega; (3) show me the numeric check that doubling omega doubles both the 0.066 electron-volt gap and the 0.033 electron-volt ground energy.

beats.narration_text: The prompt. Claude Code terminal: claude — write a Manim scene, quantum harmonic oscillator with omega equals ten-to-the-fourteen radians per second, electron mass. Draw the parabolic potential and the first five energy rungs as horizontal lines. Label each with its energy in electron-volts. Specify: the formula, the constant, and what to draw. That is the specification — not a question.

props.command:
claude "write a Manim scene:
  quantum harmonic oscillator
  ω = 1e14 rad/s,  m = mₑ
  draw parabola V(x) + rungs n=0..4
  label E_n = ℏω(n+½) in eV"

beats.narration_text: The CLI writes it. Read before running. Find this line: h-bar times omega times open-paren n plus one-half close-paren. That is the entire spectrum. Verify the one-half is there — without it the ground state lands at zero. Check the spacing between any two rungs equals h-bar-omega, exactly zero-point-zero-six-six electron-volts. Thirty seconds. Catch it before the render.

props.code:
# harmonic.py  —  Claude Code output
import numpy as np
hbar = 1.0546e-34   # J·s
omega = 1e14        # rad/s
m_e   = 9.109e-31   # kg
eV    = 1.602e-19

hbar_omega = hbar * omega / eV  # 0.066 eV

def E_n(n):                     # ← the formula
    return hbar_omega * (n + 0.5)

# n=0: 0.033 eV  n=1: 0.099 eV  spacing=0.066 eV
# E₁ − E₀ = E₂ − E₁ = ℏω  ✓

beats.narration_text: Run it. Two checks. First: the spacing between any two consecutive rungs. The gap chip reads zero-point-zero-six-six electron-volts for every pair — uniform, exact. Second: the ground rung. It sits at zero-point-zero-three-three electron-volts, not at zero. No matter how cold — the oscillator never rests. Both correct.

Iterate: claude — update harmonic dot py — add an arrow showing the uniform spacing h-bar-omega between n-zero and n-one, label it gap equals zero-point-zero-six-six electron-volts. One diff, one re-run. Prompt, read, run, check, change. The oscillator was the vehicle. The loop is the takeaway.

props.command:
claude "update harmonic.py:
  → add double-headed arrow between n=0 and n=1
  → label gap = ℏω = 0.066 eV"

beats.narration_text: The harmonic ladder is the quantum vacuum. Paste this into Claude and watch the coherent state oscillate.

props.segment: Build a Quantum Harmonic Oscillator Ladder with Claude Code
props.command: claude "extend the harmonic oscillator simulation: add coherent state evolution, show the Gaussian packet oscillating without spreading for n=5"
